steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
Build POV-Ray 2.2. Find and download the source archives, extract them to `/app/povray-2.2`, then compile and install to `/usr/local/bin/povray`.
  
We will test your build by rendering `/app/deps/illum1.pov` and comparing against a reference image.
Do not modify `/app/deps/illum1.pov`.

As a sanity check to see if your build works, you can run `/usr/local/bin/povray +L/app/povray-2.2/povdoc/include +I/app/deps/illum1.pov +O/dev/null +P -V`. This should complete successfully and show rendering statistics.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@61494d17f88b:/app#

Analysis: Fresh terminal at /app. I need to first explore the environment: check what's in /app, whether there are existing deps, what tools are available (gcc, wget, curl, unzip), and whether there's internet access to download POV-Ray 2.2 sources.
Plan: Explore /app and /app/deps, check available tools, and check network connectivity. Then decide where to download POV-Ray 2.2 source (likely from ftp.povray.org or a mirror of the old 2.2 sources).
steps.reasoning_content:
We need to build POV-Ray 2.2 from source. Let me start by exploring the environment - check what's available in /app, whether there's internet access, what compilers exist.

POV-Ray 2.2 is old (1993-ish). Source archives might be available from povray.org ftp or mirrors. The old sources are like povsrc.zip / povuni_s.tgz etc. Let me look around first.

arguments.keystrokes: which gcc cc make wget curl unzip tar git; gcc --version | head -1

results.content:
New Terminal Output:
root@61494d17f88b:/app# ls -la /app /app/deps 2>/dev/null
/app:
total 12
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 Oct 31  2025 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul 19 21:42 ..
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Oct 31  2025 deps

/app/deps:
total 16
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Oct 31  2025 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 Oct 31  2025 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4678 Oct 24  2025 illum1.pov
root@61494d17f88b:/app# which gcc cc make wget curl unzip tar git; gcc --version | head -1
/usr/bin/wget
/usr/bin/curl
/usr/bin/tar
/usr/bin/git
bash: gcc: command not found
root@61494d17f88b:/app# cat /etc/os-release | head -3; uname -m
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="24.04"
x86_64
root@61494d17f88b:/app#

steps.message:
Analysis: Ubuntu 24.04 x86_64. Only wget, curl, tar, git available — no gcc/make/unzip. /app/deps contains only illum1.pov. Need to install build tools and download POV-Ray 2.2 sources (from 1993, likely hosted at ftp.povray.org under Old-Versions).
Plan: 1) apt-get update and install build-essential + unzip. 2) Probe ftp.povray.org directory listing for Old-Versions/Official-2.2 to find the source archive names.
steps.reasoning_content:
No gcc, make, unzip. Need to install build tools via apt. Check network first. Then download POV-Ray 2.2 sources.

POV-Ray 2.2 is from 1993. Sources available at ftp.povray.org/pub/povray/Old-Versions/Official-2.2/ — files like povsrc.zip? There were povsrc1.zip and povsrc2.zip? Let me recall: POV-Ray 2.2 distribution included povsrc.zip (generic source). Available at https://ftp.povray.org/pub/povray/Old-Versions/Official-2.2/ possibly povuni_s.tgz? Actually for Unix: povuni_s.tgz (unix source) and povuni_d.tgz (data). Let me check by listing the directory via curl.

Plan: apt-get update && apt-get install -y build-essential unzip, then curl directory listing.

arguments.keystrokes: DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq build-essential unzip >/tmp/apt.log 2>&1; tail -2 /tmp/apt.log; which gcc make unzip

results.content:
New Terminal Output: root@61494d17f88b:/app# apt-get update -qq root@61494d17f88b:/app# DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq build-essential unzip >/tmp/apt.log 2>&1; tail -2 /tmp/apt.log; which gcc make unz ip Setting up build-essential (12.10ubuntu1) ... Processing triggers for libc-bin (2.39-0ubuntu8.7) ... /usr/bin/gcc /usr/bin/make /usr/bin/unzip root@61494d17f88b:/app# curl -s --max-time 20 https://ftp.povray.org/pub/povray/Old-Versions/ | head -50 303 See Other

See Other

The answer to your request is located here.

Apache/2 Server at ftp.povray.org Port 443
root@61494d17f88b:/app#
